apiOrSchemaChanges: internal/engine: +type Tier int (None/Generic/Lint/Parse/Full), +func TierForLanguage(string) Tier, +var languageTiers map[string]Tier
fileMap: internal/discovery/generic.go — modify — add exported KnownLanguages() to expose deduplicated language IDs from certifiableExts
internal/engine/language_tiers.go — create — Tier enum, static registry of 22 language→tier mappings, TierForLanguage helper
internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go — create — three tests: exhaustive coverage, unknown fallback, Swift regression pin
fixApproach: Add a minimal exported `KnownLanguages()` function to `internal/discovery/generic.go` so the engine test can enumerate all known language IDs. Then create `internal/engine/language_tiers.go` with a `Tier` enum (None=0, Generic, Lint, Parse, Full), a static `map[string]Tier` registry covering all 22 languages, and a `TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier` helper defaulting to TierGeneric. Finally, create `internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go` with three tests: exhaustive coverage of all discovery-known languages, the unknown-language fail-safe, and a Swift-is-Generic regression pin.
steps: Add exported `KnownLanguages() []string` to `internal/discovery/generic.go` deduplicating and sorting keys from `certifiableExts`
Create `internal/engine/language_tiers.go`: define `Tier` iota enum (None, Generic, Lint, Parse, Full) with a `String()` method

Define the static registry `var languageTiers = map[string]Tier{…}` mapping all 22 language IDs to their tiers: go/ts/js/py/rs → Full, all others (rb,java,kt,sh,c,cpp,cs,swift,php,elixir,erlang,scala,lua,r,zig,sql,proto) → Generic
Implement `func TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier` returning the registry entry or TierGeneric for unknowns
Create `internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go` with TestTierRegistry_AllKnownLanguagesHaveTiers (calls discovery.KnownLanguages(), asserts every ID has a non-zero tier entry)
Add TestTierRegistry_SwiftIsGeneric (asserts TierForLanguage("swift") == TierGeneric as regression pin)
Run `go test ./... -count=1` to verify all tests pass
summary: Create a language-tier registry (None/Generic/Lint/Parse/Full) mapping all 22 known language IDs to capability tiers, with a CI-enforcing test that fails when a new extension is added without a tier entry.
